body.title: Eliminate row scans: push_sources + narrative_engine should read from hot attractor state
body.result: T-no-row-scans complete: added SQL-based filtering methods (get_by_type_and_source, get_procedural_by_metadata_key) and updated narrative_engine, push_sources, system_prompt to use SQL filtering instead of fetch-then-filter patterns. All 354 tests passing. Commit: cfdedda
body.description: push_sources fetches all memories of all types then filters by activation_count in Python (17K+ rows per cycle). narrative_engine fetches 500 EPISODICs and filters in Python for source=narrative_engine. Both are reconstructing state the runtime already maintains. Short-term fix: replace with SQL queries scoped to activation threshold using existing idx_mem_activation index. Real fix: read from hot_attractors/TWM — nodes with high activation are already warm, no scan needed. Architectural principle: no row searches. Every query starts from a hot node and traverses edges. Trees all the way down.
